title: Separate Even and Odd values in a linked list

content.problemStatement:
Chef has a linked list. He wants to modify the linked list such that all the even values appear before the odd values without changing their relative order. But he's too busy now and asked you to do the job for him.

Can you do it?


Input Format

First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of every test case contains an integer NNN - the length of array.
The second line of every test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,..,AN A_1,A_2,..,A_NA1​,A2​,..,AN​ denoting the integers in the linked list.
You don't need to read or print anything. Just complete the function rearrange() which takes the head of the linked list as input.


Output Format
Return the head of the linked list after rearrangement.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 5 * 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the linked list. The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the node values in order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the rearranged list: all even values first (preserving their original relative order) followed by all odd values (also preserving their original relative order). Values are separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: evens [2] come before odds [1,3] → 2 1 3.\nTest case 2: evens [6,8] before odds [1,7] → 6 8 1 7.\nTest case 3: evens [2,4] before odds [1,3] → 2 4 1 3.
Evens are 4 and 2 (preserving order), odds are 5,3,1. Combined → 4 2 5 3 1.
Only one odd element, list remains unchanged.

solution.approach: Traverse the list once while storing even values in one container and odd values in another. Because we only need to preserve the original order inside each group, simple appends are enough. After the traversal concatenate the even container followed by the odd container and output the result. This works for an actual linked list as well – we could rebuild the next pointers, but for the purpose of the problem we only need to print the values, so using arrays is sufficient and runs in linear time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case – each element is inspected exactly once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the two auxiliary containers holding evens and odds.
